title: Lanternwood Folk Fest 2026 program booklet: sponsor-logo prep + performer-photo grade + InDesign data-merge to press-ready PDF
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Freelancer.com brief "Event Program Booklet / Brochure Design": the client is "producing a booklet-style brochure that will double as the official program guide for our upcoming event"; "All copy, sponsor logos and a rough page sequence will be in your hands as soon as we start"; the ask is "layout of the full multi-page booklet, including front/back covers", "careful proofreading, alignment and image optimisation for print", and "delivery of a press-ready PD[F]"; "Every logo must appear crisp, proportionate and consistently placed, with plenty of white space so partners look their best." REASONABLY ENRICHED to make it a runnable Adobe-only benchmark: the original brief implies a from-scratch multi-page layout AND defining a fresh colour/type identity, which is NOT in connector scope (no from-scratch layout composition or type-system design; document_merge_data_layout / document_render_layout only BIND/EXPORT a GENUINE user-authored .indd carrying real merge fields). So the client is modeled as supplying the authored InDesign program-booklet template (with the colour scheme and type hierarchy already established, per a normal second-round freelancer hand-off), a CSV of the performance schedule + performer bios (one row per set), a CSV of sponsor tiers, the raw sponsor logo files, and raw performer press photos. The Adobe chain then does the genuinely-doable heavy lifting the brief actually rewards: making every sponsor logo crisp and print-safe (vectorize the flat raster ones, lift the busy-background ones onto transparent PNGs), grading the dull/tilted performer press photos so they look their best, sourcing a subtle paper/kraft texture for the covers, uploading all prepped assets, then running the client's authored .indd through a genuine InDesign data-merge against the schedule and sponsor CSVs so each set/bio/sponsor binds into the template, and exporting a press-ready PDF plus a screen-quality PDF for the event app. The "sponsor logos... crisp, proportionate and consistently placed" instruction is honored literally via vectorize + background-lift + the merged sponsor grid. No deliverable was invented beyond the program booklet the brief asks for.
one_line_ask: Prep the Lanternwood Folk Fest sponsor logos (vectorize / transparent-cut) and grade the performer press photos, source a kraft cover texture, then run the client's authored multi-page InDesign booklet template through two data-merge passes against the schedule and sponsor CSVs to produce a press-ready PDF and a screen-quality PDF program guide.
full_brief: Lanternwood Folk Fest, a fictional two-day roots-and-Americana music festival, needs its official program booklet finished for print and for the event app. The organizer has handed off an authored multi-page InDesign booklet template (folkfest_program_template.indd) that already carries the festival's established warm-cream / lantern-amber colour scheme and type hierarchy, with real InDesign data-merge fields laid out across the interior spreads (<<day>>, <<stage>>, <<set_time>>, <<artist>>, <<bio>>, <<artist_photo>> for the schedule pages, and <<sponsor_name>>, <<sponsor_tier>>, <<sponsor_logo>> for the sponsor grid on the back-cover spread), plus front/back covers with a full-bleed frame for a kraft-paper texture and a placeholder for the festival wordmark. Along with the template the organizer supplies: a schedule CSV (one row per performance set: day, stage, set time, artist, one-line bio, and the filename of that artist's press photo), a sponsor CSV (sponsor name, tier — Presenting / Stage / Community — and the filename of that sponsor's logo), a folder of raw sponsor logo files (some are clean flat rasters, some are photos of the logo on a busy background, some are already reasonable PNGs), and a folder of raw performer press photos that are unappetizing as-shot: slightly tilted, dull and flat, mixed white-balance because they came from different photographers. The job: (1) make every sponsor logo crisp and print-safe per the brief — vectorize the flat raster logos into clean SVGs so they stay sharp at any size, and lift the busy-background logos onto clean transparent PNGs so they sit consistently on the cream page; (2) grade the performer press photos so the artists look their best — straighten the tilted ones, auto-tone the flat exposure, normalize the mixed white balance toward the festival's warm look, push vibrance, and crop each to the uniform portrait ratio the schedule frames expect; (3) license a subtle kraft/recycled-paper texture from Adobe Stock to back the front and back covers; (4) upload all the prepped assets, then run the client's authored .indd through the InDesign data-merge against the schedule CSV (interior schedule/bio pages) and then against the sponsor CSV (back-cover sponsor grid) so every set, bio, artist photo, sponsor name, tier and logo binds into the correct frame; (5) export the merged booklet to a press-ready CMYK PDF with bleed/crop marks for the printer and a lighter RGB screen-quality PDF for the event app. All copy stays exactly as supplied in the CSVs — the design work is the layout binding, image optimization and logo prep the brief asks for, nothing rewritten.

inputs.name: folkfest_program_template.indd
inputs.gen_prompt: AUTHORED DELIVERABLE (cannot be model-generated): a genuine multi-page Adobe InDesign document (.indd/.idml) for a saddle-stitched A5 portrait Lanternwood Folk Fest program booklet in the festival's established warm-cream / lantern-amber identity. Interior schedule spreads must contain REAL InDesign data-merge placeholder fields named <<day>>, <<stage>>, <<set_time>>, <<artist>>, <<bio>> and an image-merge frame <<artist_photo>> (sized to a 4:5 portrait ratio), repeated per set. A back-cover sponsor grid must contain <<sponsor_name>>, <<sponsor_tier>> and an image-merge frame <<sponsor_logo>>. Front and back covers must include a full-bleed background frame for the kraft texture and a placeholder frame for the festival wordmark. Authored by hand in InDesign so the data-merge fields actually bind; a PDF-converted .indd with literal baked text would NOT bind.
inputs.realism_notes: Modeled as the client's own draft template with the colour scheme and type hierarchy already defined (a normal second-round freelancer hand-off). document_merge_data_layout is [T] and REQUIRES a user-authored .indd carrying real merge fields — it cannot be connector-generated, so it is a supplied input, not a chain output. Because the tool accepts a single dataSourceUrl per call, the template is bound in two passes (schedule fields, then sponsor-grid fields).

inputs.name: folkfest_schedule.csv
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a CSV with columns day,stage,set_time,artist,bio,artist_photo holding ~14 performance-set rows across a two-day roots/Americana festival: day is 'Saturday' or 'Sunday'; stage is 'Lantern Stage','Willow Stage' or 'Barn Stage'; set_time like '2:30 PM'; artist is a fictional folk/Americana act (e.g. 'The Copper Kettle Trio','Marigold & the Wandering Sons'); bio is one appetizing sentence; artist_photo is the filename of that act's press photo (e.g. 'press_copper_kettle.jpg'). Column names must exactly match the InDesign merge fields <<day>>,<<stage>>,<<set_time>>,<<artist>>,<<bio>>,<<artist_photo>>.
inputs.realism_notes: Honors the brief's 'all copy... will be in your hands'; column headers match the authored .indd schedule merge fields so the [T] data-merge binds; artist_photo column references the graded press photos by filename via additionalImageFiles.

inputs.name: folkfest_sponsors.csv
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a CSV with columns sponsor_name,sponsor_tier,sponsor_logo holding ~9 fictional sponsor rows: sponsor_name like 'Harbormill Coffee Roasters','Cedarline Outfitters','Riverbend Credit Union'; sponsor_tier is 'Presenting','Stage' or 'Community'; sponsor_logo is the filename of that sponsor's logo file (e.g. 'logo_harbormill.svg'). Column names must exactly match the InDesign merge fields <<sponsor_name>>,<<sponsor_tier>>,<<sponsor_logo>>.
inputs.realism_notes: Drives the back-cover sponsor grid; headers match the authored .indd sponsor merge fields; sponsor_logo column references the prepped logo files by filename so 'every logo appears crisp, proportionate and consistently placed'. Bound in a separate merge pass because the tool takes one CSV per call.

inputs.name: logo_harbormill_flat.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat two-color sponsor wordmark logo reading 'Harbormill Coffee Roasters' with a small coffee-bean-and-anchor emblem, solid dark-teal and cream, on a transparent background, crisp flat high-contrast raster PNG, no gradients, clean edges — suitable for vectorizing.
inputs.realism_notes: gemini-3-pro-image for clean legible logo text; flat and high-contrast so image_vectorize produces a clean print-safe SVG — represents the 'clean flat raster' subset of the supplied logos.

inputs.name: logo_cedarline_flat.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat single-color sponsor logo reading 'Cedarline Outfitters' with a simple pine-tree mark, solid forest green on transparent background, crisp flat high-contrast raster PNG, no gradients, clean vector-ready edges.
inputs.realism_notes: Second flat-raster logo for the vectorize sub-chain; single-color line art vectorizes cleanly to a scalable SVG.

inputs.name: logo_riverbend_on_signage.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal phone snapshot of the 'Riverbend Credit Union' logo printed on a glass storefront window, shot at a slight angle with reflections and a blurred street visible through the glass behind it. The logo itself (a blue river-wave mark and navy wordmark) is fully readable and unoccluded, but sits on a busy real-world background. Natural daylight, candid documentary look.
inputs.realism_notes: Photographed-on-background logo so the pipeline must LIFT it onto a clean transparent PNG (image_select_by_prompt + remove_background) rather than vectorize; logo is fully visible/unoccluded so no reconstruction/inpainting is needed.

inputs.name: press_copper_kettle.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal press photo of a three-piece folk/Americana band 'The Copper Kettle Trio' — banjo, upright bass and acoustic guitar — standing outside a weathered barn. Slightly tilted horizon (about 3 degrees), flat overcast lighting, slightly cool/greyish white balance, real skin texture and clothing detail, candid documentary feel, no studio gloss.
inputs.realism_notes: Deliberately imperfect (tilt, flat tone, cool WB) so the grade chain has real work; gpt-image-2 for photoreal people.

inputs.name: press_marigold_sons.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal press photo of a folk duo 'Marigold & the Wandering Sons' seated on porch steps with a fiddle and a guitar. Underexposed dull shadows, warm-but-muddy indoor tungsten cast, slightly soft, candid look. Real texture, no CGI gloss.
inputs.realism_notes: Different photographer's mixed/muddy white balance justifies auto-tone + temperature normalization so both press photos match the festival warm look.

inputs.name: press_hollow_pines.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal press photo of a solo singer-songwriter 'Hollow Pines' holding an acoustic guitar in a field at golden hour, but the shot is flat and slightly overexposed with a washed sky and a tilted horizon. Real detail, candid, no studio polish.
inputs.realism_notes: Third performer portrait; flat/overexposed + tilt so the same straighten→tone→temperature→vibrance→crop pipeline applies consistently across all press photos.

outputs.name: Lanternwood_Program_press.pdf
outputs.spec: Press-ready CMYK PDF of the full multi-page A5 booklet exported from the twice-merged InDesign document, with bleed and crop marks; schedule/bio/sponsor copy bound from the CSVs, graded performer photos + prepped sponsor logos + kraft cover texture placed; ~300 DPI print quality for the saddle-stitch printer. Also delivered as Lanternwood_Program_screen.pdf: Lighter RGB screen-quality PDF of the same bound booklet (no crop marks, downsampled images) for the festival event app and email distribution.

outputs.name: graded_performer_photos
outputs.spec: Three straightened, auto-toned, white-balance-normalized, vibrance-boosted performer press photos, each cropped to the uniform 4:5 portrait ratio the schedule frames expect.

outputs.name: sponsor_logos_prepped
outputs.spec: Sponsor logos made print-safe: the two flat-raster logos delivered as clean scalable SVGs (vectorized), and the on-signage logo lifted onto a clean transparent-background PNG so all sit consistently in the back-cover sponsor grid.

connector_workflow.note: Search Adobe Stock for a subtle kraft/recycled-paper texture to back the front and back covers (low-res candidates).
License and download the chosen kraft-paper texture at full-res (license BEFORE use); becomes the cover background asset.
Vectorize the flat Harbormill raster logo into a clean SVG so it stays razor-sharp at any print size.
Vectorize the flat single-color Cedarline logo into a clean scalable SVG.
Select just the Riverbend logo mark+wordmark off the busy storefront-glass background (logo is fully visible/unoccluded).
Lift the selected Riverbend logo onto a clean transparent PNG so it sits consistently on the cream sponsor grid (no reconstruction — the logo is unoccluded).
Level the tilted horizon on the Copper Kettle Trio press photo.
Auto-tone the straightened photo to fix the flat/dull overcast exposure.
Warm the cool/greyish white balance toward the festival's warm look.
Push vibrance so the band and instruments read well on the printed page.
Crop/resize to the 4:5 portrait frame the schedule template expects (same target for all press photos).
Run the Marigold duo photo through the same grade pipeline starting with auto-tone (fixes the underexposed shadows).
Normalize the muddy tungsten cast to match the festival warm white balance.
Lift vibrance so the duo photo matches the graded look of the others.
Crop the Marigold photo to the matching 4:5 schedule frame.
Level the tilted horizon on the Hollow Pines golden-hour photo.
Auto-tone to pull back the washed/overexposed sky and flat contrast.
Crop the Hollow Pines photo to the 4:5 frame so all three performer portraits match.
Create a Creative Cloud folder to hold all prepped booklet assets before upload/merge.

connector_workflow.inputs_from: folkfest_asset_folder, harbormill_svg, cedarline_svg, riverbend_logo_transparent_png, copper_cropped_4x5, marigold_cropped_4x5, hollow_cropped_4x5, kraft_texture_fullres
connector_workflow.note: Initialize an upload session for all prepped assets (vector logos, transparent logo, graded photos, cover texture) into the folder.
Finalize the upload; returns asset URNs (and presigned URLs) the InDesign data-merge references by filename via additionalImageFiles.

First data-merge pass: bind the schedule rows (day/stage/set_time/artist/bio + the graded artist_photo images) into the interior schedule frames of the client's authored .indd (which carries real <<fields>>). Single dataSourceUrl per call, so schedule and sponsors are two passes. [T] requires the user-authored template.

Second data-merge pass over the schedule-merged .indd: bind the sponsor rows (sponsor_name/sponsor_tier + prepped sponsor_logo SVGs/PNG) into the back-cover sponsor grid so every logo sits crisp and consistently placed. Output is the fully-bound booklet .indd.
Export the fully-merged booklet to a press-ready ~300 DPI CMYK PDF with bleed/crop marks for the saddle-stitch printer.
Export the same fully-merged booklet to a lighter RGB screen-quality PDF for the event app and email distribution.

chaining_note: Strict output→input chaining throughout. Two logo sub-chains: the flat-raster logos vectorize directly (logo_harbormill_flat.png→vectorize(3); logo_cedarline_flat.png→vectorize(4)), while the on-signage logo runs a two-link lift (logo_riverbend_on_signage.jpg→select_by_prompt(5)→remove_background(6)). Three performer photos each run their own grade→crop sub-chain: press_copper_kettle.jpg→straighten(7)→auto_tone(8)→temperature(9)→vibrance(10)→crop(11); press_marigold_sons.jpg→auto_tone(12)→temperature(13)→vibrance(14)→crop(15); press_hollow_pines.jpg→straighten(16)→auto_tone(17)→crop(18). The kraft texture chains search(1)→license(2). A CC folder is created(19), then all prepped outputs (the two SVGs, the transparent logo PNG, the three 4:5 photos, the kraft texture) converge into the upload session(20)→finalize(21). The finalized URNs plus the authored folkfest_program_template.indd and the schedule CSV feed the first data-merge pass(22); its output .indd plus the sponsor CSV and the same URNs feed the second data-merge pass(23), giving one fully-bound booklet .indd. That single .indd is the source for both exports — press PDF(24) and screen PDF(25). Two merge passes are used because document_merge_data_layout binds a single dataSourceUrl CSV per call; nothing after step 1/2 (search) and the raw input loads originates outside the chain — the final booklet emerges only from the assembled chain.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 25 connector calls across 3 tool groups (image_* raster, asset_* stock/storage/folders, document_* InDesign), 15 distinct Adobe tools — far above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from prepping a mixed bag of sponsor logos two different correct ways (vectorize the flat rasters vs. select+lift the photographed one), running three separate performer photos through a consistent multi-step grade pipeline to a uniform crop, sourcing+licensing a cover texture, organizing everything into a CC folder, then converging it all into a genuine TWO-pass InDesign [T] data-merge (schedule CSV then sponsor CSV, because the tool binds one dataSourceUrl per call) and dual PDF export (press CMYK + screen RGB). It respects every hard limit: no generative fill / object-removal-with-reconstruction / text-to-image / from-scratch layout composition / upscaling; image_fill_area is unused; the layout is NOT composed by the connector — merge_data_layout/render_layout only BIND/EXPORT the client's authored .indd, which is listed as a supplied input; the on-signage logo is fully visible so remove_background is a clean lift, not inpainting; all copy stays verbatim from the supplied CSVs.

reverify.banned_capability_check: None of the banned capabilities are used. No generative-expand/outpaint, no generative fill of new content, no text-to-image / from-scratch art or logo, no upscaling/super-resolution, no object-removal-with-reconstruction (inpainting), no bespoke font design, no unreachable external account. image_fill_area is NOT used.
reverify.notes: CORRECTED from the authored draft: the original single document_merge_data_layout call passed TWO CSVs (schedule + sponsors), but the tool binds a single dataSourceUrl per call — split into two sequential [T] merge passes (n22 schedule pages, n23 sponsor grid over the schedule-merged .indd). Both passes are correctly tagged [T]: the multi-page booklet layout, colour scheme and type hierarchy are all supplied in the user-authored folkfest_program_template.indd (kind:data, gen_model:'n/a-authored-asset') carrying real <<fields>>; the connector only BINDS data and EXPORTS — it does not compose the layout or invent a design identity, so the brief's from-scratch/identity-design portion is deliberately supplied, not generated. Each CSV's headers exactly match its template merge fields so the merge binds, and the graded/prepped images are referenced by filename via the tool's additionalImageFiles map; the spec notes a PDF-converted .indd with baked text would not bind. image_vectorize (n3,n4) runs only on flat high-contrast raster logos (legitimate vectorize, not logo design). image_select_by_prompt(n5)+image_remove_background(n6) lift the Riverbend logo, which is fully visible and unoccluded on the signage, so this is a clean cutout to transparent PNG — no reconstruction/inpainting of hidden pixels. The performer grade steps (straighten/auto_tone/temperature/vibrance/crop) are standard raster adjustments. asset_search here is the Adobe asset/stock search (distinct from the Express-widget search_design), and asset_license_and_download_stock licenses before use. All copy is bound verbatim from the supplied CSVs — nothing is rewritten. All 25 steps server=='adobe'; 15 distinct Adobe tools (>=6), 25 calls (>=10) — qualifies as long-horizon flagship.
